untranslatable_reason: Signal requires tracking volatility state from exit day and comparing current volatility to one-quarter of that historical value. The engine lacks: (1) a mechanism to store and reference volatility at trade exit time, (2) an operator to compute fractional relationships (one-quarter), and (3) context-dependent directional logic that depends on the direction of the just-exited trade. These require stateful logic beyond the allowed indicator/operator vocabulary.
translation_notes: This is a reentry signal with memory requirements. It needs to: capture hvol_20 on exit day, compute exit_day_hvol * 0.25 as a threshold, then reenter when current hvol_20 falls below that threshold. The direction of reentry mirrors the exited trade direction. Standard backtest engines would need custom variables or callbacks to track exit-time volatility state and apply fractional thresholds.
